[PDF_PAGE 35 | score=4] King Arthur Merlin King Arthur is the legendary ruler who united Britain and beal hack her Saxon in­ vaders. He also estab lished a realm of vir­ tue, Logres . thai is the spiritual counterpa rt of his material kingdom. Ar­ thur is not on ly the principal defender of Britain , but the embodiment of her ethical ideals as well. Arthur's heritage and remarkable achievements have al­ ready been discussed in the introduction to this section, so it shall surrice here to say that he is a savior king in every sense of the word. He rules his realm, the united kingdoms of Brit­ ain. with compassion, wisdom . and , when need be, unop­ posable might. Paladin 16. Bard 5 Sir 18152 lnt 17 AC -1 HP147 IAT2 
[PDF_PAGE 56 | score=4] Nezahualcoytl By many accounts, Nezahualcoytl was the greatest priest-king to ru le in the Valley of Mexico. He was the ruler of Texcoco, an uneasy ally of the ea rly Aztecs. He began '--c----" his early life in exile from political vengeance , then returned to power and restored the fortunes of his people. He was a just. able adminis trator , a capable gene ral, and an avid as­ tronomer. Nezahua1coyt l believed in an all-powerful deity from which the power of the other gods was manifes ted, Fighte r 14, P ries t 12 Sir 17 Dex 18 Con 18 Int 17 Wis 18 Cha 16 AC4 MRnii MV12 HP 96 AL ng THACo 6 NAT 2 Dmg Id611d6 (sword) +1 Spells: 1) command, remove fear. detect magic, detect poison , detect sn
[PDF_PAGE 33 | score=3] Arthurian Heroes The material component of impersonation is a drop of blood, lock of hair. or cherished possession belonging to the person who is to be impersonated. Revelation (Greater Divination) Sixth Level Wizard Fifth level Priest (Divination) Range: Line of sight Components: V.S Duration: 1 round Casting Time: 1 round Area of Effect: Self Saving Throw: None By casting a revelation spell, a wizard immediately under~ stands, in unerring detail. the complete meaning of an omen or portent. This spell can only be used when the spellcaster sees the omen, and must be used while the portent is remains visible. Revelation cannot be used to predict the future. ex­ cept as indicated by the porten
[PDF_PAGE 55 | score=3] Centeotl (Intermediate god) Ixtlilton (lesser god) Cenleoll is the goddess of agricultu re. na· ture , and com in particular (she shares this last duty with a male counterpart, Cinlec!)). She is a beneficent deity who watches over food crops. but is also the mistress of pain and illness. and has a habit of using her physical charms to lure men to their death. Unlike Tlazoteotl. she seems to do this not out of maliciousness . but because such victims are necessary for the fertility of crops. Role-playing Notes: Centrot] watches over the harvest, dictating how bountiful it will be based upon the devotion of her worshipers. Although she needs the lives of young men to ensure her cycle of fertil
[PDF_PAGE 58 | score=3] Celtic History Perhaps more so than any othe r cultur e addressed in this volume, the Celts are very difficult to pin down as a single cohesive nation. Their unusual beginnings and their lack of any centralized gover nmental structu re mark them as unique, somehow outside the bounds of what we today con­ sider to be a count ry or state. The historical record tells us thai Celtic unity was based strict ly on culture , not on race, language. or heritage. Of course, our knowledge of the ancient Celts is distorted as we look through the romanticism of 18th and 19th century scholar s. At every tum in ou r study, we have to ca refully judge the sou rce of information and decide whether or not that
[PDF_PAGE 104 | score=3] Though it flourished 2.500 years ago, no other civilization has had as much influence on Ihe spirit of the western world as ancient Greece. OUf most basic and dearly held convic­ tions, such as a man's right to liberty, to be ruled by a demo­ cratic government, and the inherent worth of the individual. are directly descended from Greek thought. Geographically, ancient Greece was very much Ihe same as modern Greece. Located al the southern lip of the Balkan land mass, it is a region of hard. limestone mountains sepa­ rated by deep valleys. and cut almost in two by the narrow Strait of Co rinth . To Ihe east, hundreds of isles dot the clear blue Aegean sea, and the mighty island of Crete marks
[PDF_PAGE 152 | score=3] • • Amatsu·Mikaboshi, the "augus t star of ,----:: =::- -, heaven," is the god of evil. Also known as Ama·no-Kagaseo, the "brilliant male," he sees and sanctions all things done under cover of darkness. He has the power to look into the hearts of men and women, and, if he finds too much evil there. to make them his. Because most people are basically good, Amatsu -Mikaboshi is not par­ ticularly powerfu l. But he is cunning, and has gathered more worshipers than people suspect. In his true form, Amalsu­ Mikaboshi is a dark shadow that can never quite be seen. Ro le·pla ying Noles: Amatsu-Mikaboshi gathers wor­ shipers the old fashioned way: he entraps them. After finding a potential worshiper
[PDF_PAGE 170 | score=3] Water Cobra The water cobra is a bold attacker, nor· mally feeding on fish and small mammals. When threatened its first attack is towards the head of the creature. Its first two strikes are lightning quick bites designed to inject a poison into its prey. After it has made two successful attacks the snake will dive and wail to determine if the creature it at­ tacked is dead. If its victim lives, there is a 75% chance that the snake will retreat to find easier prey. Land versions of this cobra also have the ability 10 change skin color to perfectly blend in with its surroundings from moment to moment. The land version also has the ability to coil and leap up to five feel towards the intended p
